module-3-sales-leaderboard-project.label: Module 3 Sales Leaderboard Project
module-3-sales-leaderboard-project.summary: Turn a rough ranking query into a clean leaderboard.

mp-1-rank-by-price.title: Rank the products by price
mp-1-rank-by-price.prompt: Start the leaderboard by returning `name` and `price` from `products`, ordered so the most expensive products appear first.
mp-1-rank-by-price.hint: Use DESC instead of ASC for the price sort.

help.concept: Ranking queries become easier to read when the most important rows appear first.
help.hint_1: Keep the selected columns the same.
help.hint_2: Sort with `ORDER BY price DESC`, then rerun the query.

mp-1-rank-by-price.starterCode:
-- Returning name and price from products, ordered so the most expensive products appear first.
SELECT
  name,
  price
FROM products
ORDER BY price ASC;

mp-2-add-stock-tiebreaker.title: Add a stock tiebreaker
mp-2-add-stock-tiebreaker.prompt: Add `stock` to the result, and sort by `price` descending with `stock` ascending as the second sort.
mp-2-add-stock-tiebreaker.hint: Use two columns in the ORDER BY clause.

help.concept: A secondary sort helps you break ties or make a leaderboard easier to scan.
help.hint_1: Add `stock` to the SELECT list first.
help.hint_2: Use `ORDER BY price DESC, stock ASC`, then rerun the query.

mp-2-add-stock-tiebreaker.starterCode:
-- Keep the ranking above.
-- Add stock to the result and sort stock ASC after price DESC.
SELECT
  name,
  price
FROM products
ORDER BY price DESC;

mp-3-finish-leaderboard.title: Finish the top-five leaderboard
mp-3-finish-leaderboard.prompt: Limit the ranked report to the top 5 rows.
mp-3-finish-leaderboard.hint: Add one LIMIT line after the ORDER BY clause.

help.concept: A limit turns a broad ranking into a short report someone can scan quickly.
help.hint_1: Do not change the selected columns or the sort order.
help.hint_2: Add `LIMIT 5;` at the end of the query, then rerun it.

mp-3-finish-leaderboard.starterCode:
-- Keep the ranking above.
-- Limit the leaderboard to 5 rows.
SELECT
  name,
  price,
  stock
FROM products
ORDER BY price DESC, stock ASC;

try-module-3-sales-leaderboard-project-s.title: Query name and price from products
try-module-3-sales-leaderboard-project-s.prompt: Scenario: The sales team needs a sorted result that answers a practical leaderboard question. Write a query that returns `name`, `price` from the `products` table. Write the query in the SQL editor, click Run query, and check the results table.
try-module-3-sales-leaderboard-project-s.hint: Use the query pattern from the lesson, then adjust the selected columns, table, or filter for this scenario.

help.concept: This SQL exercise checks whether your query returns the requested result.
help.hint_1: Check the selected columns and table name in the SQL editor.
help.hint_2: Run the query and compare the result table with the expected result.

try-module-3-sales-leaderboard-project-s.starterCode:
-- Return name, price from the products table.
SELECT
  name,
  price
FROM products
ORDER BY price ASC;

try_module_3_sales_leaderboard_project_s.prompt: Scenario: The sales team needs a sorted result that answers a practical leaderboard question. Write a query that returns `name`, `price` from the `products` table. Write the query in the SQL editor, click Run query, and check the results table.

sketch_project_brief.title: Project brief
sketch_project_brief.bodyMarkdown:
You are building a small leaderboard a teammate can scan quickly.

Start with a simple ranking, then make it easier to read with better sorting and a tighter result set.

The finished report should look like a clear top-five list.
